summary: Added a GitHub Pages deployment path for a generated `ctx.html` context file. The PR extends `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` with a `--render`/`-r` option that writes `build/ctx.html` using a new `tools/ctx_template.html`, then updates `.github/workflows/publish.yml` to install Python dependencies, run `python tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr`, and copy the rendered HTML into the Doxygen Pages artifact.
what_changed:
Added `tools/ctx_template.html`, a simple HTML wrapper containing a collapsible `
{ctx}
` and a copy-to-clipboard button.
Added a `-r`/`--render` argparse flag to `m2ctx.py` with help text `render the context to html`.
When `--render` is passed, `m2ctx.py` reads the template, substitutes `{ctx}` with the generated context output via `template.format(ctx=output)`, and writes `build/ctx.html`.

Updated `.github/workflows/publish.yml` to set up Python 3.11 with pip caching, install `requirements.txt`, run `python tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr`, and copy `build/ctx.html` into `build/doxygen/html` before uploading the Pages artifact.
smart_moves: Reused the existing `m2ctx.py` generation flow instead of adding a separate context-rendering script.
Made HTML rendering opt-in with `--render`, preserving existing CLI behavior unless the new flag is requested.
Integrated `ctx.html` into the existing GitHub Pages/Doxygen publishing workflow, avoiding a separate deployment target.
Used `-pqr` in CI so the generated context preserves preprocessor directives, stays quiet in logs, and renders to HTML in one command.
Added a copy-to-clipboard button to make the generated context easy to reuse for decompilation tooling or external review.
decomp_lessons: Frequently used generated context artifacts can be published automatically from `master` so contributors do not need to regenerate them locally for every lookup.
For decomp tooling outputs, add machine-friendly generation first and wrap it with a minimal human-friendly HTML template rather than duplicating generation logic.
Keep new tooling behavior behind explicit CLI flags to avoid surprising existing local workflows.
CI-generated context pages are useful for header/type work because they centralize the current project context in an easily shareable artifact.
naming_conventions: The generated header/source paths use `ctx.h` and `ctx.c`; the rendered HTML follows the same stem as `ctx.html`.
The render-related path variables are named directly as `render_path` and `template_path`.
The new CLI flag uses both short and long forms: `-r` and `--render`.
The HTML template file is named `tools/ctx_template.html`, matching the generated context abbreviation `ctx`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text noted that the result looked like a hosted example at `https://ribbanya.github.io/melee/ctx.html`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` render behavior or escape handling for HTML output.
Find PRs that consume or reference the published `ctx.html` artifact.
Check whether later workflow changes kept `python tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr` in the Pages deployment.
Search for later improvements to `ctx_template.html`, especially HTML escaping, styling, or clipboard UX.
